nativeRange.habitat: Exposed seaward reefs and occasionally clear lagoons or channels; tends to form aggregations along the upper edge of reef slopes over live coral and rubble at depths of 0–37 m.

diet.notes: In the wild, has been documented feeding primarily on zooplankton. In captivity, generally accepts a varied diet including vitamin-enriched frozen mysis shrimp, frozen brine shrimp, finely crushed flake foods, and small pellets. Feeding multiple times daily in small amounts is commonly recommended to mimic their natural plankton-feeding behavior.

summary: One of the smallest chromis species in the hobby, the Acares Midget Reef Chromis tends to form active aggregations on Pacific seaward reefs. Generally considered hardy and beginner-friendly; is commonly considered reef-safe.
careNotes:
Generally advised to keep in groups of 3 or more, with odd-numbered groups sometimes suggested to reduce hierarchical aggression. Conspecific squabbling has been known to occur in smaller groups as dominant individuals establish a pecking order. Tends to occupy mid-water swimming zones and appreciates open swimming space above a rockwork base.

Temperature note: LiveAquaria recommends 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C) while FishBase wild habitat data indicates 25.4–28.4°C; the averaged range of 24.0–27.0°C is used here, reflecting a moderate cross-source disagreement. Aquarists have successfully kept this species across the full overlap range.

Pycnochromis acares is one of the smallest members of the Chrominae subfamily, reaching a maximum of around 4.0 cm standard length (approximately 5 cm total length) — notably smaller than Chromis viridis (Blue-Green Chromis). FishBase classifies this species as uncommon in occurrence. The species has been known to be sold under the names 'Midget Chromis' and 'Midget Reef Chromis' in the trade.
breedingNotes: Oviparous with benthic eggs that adhere to the substrate. Distinct pairing has been documented during spawning, with males guarding and aerating eggs. Captive breeding is rarely attempted and detailed reports from home aquaria are limited.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (4.0 cm SL confirmed), native range (Mariana Islands to Hawaii/Society Islands, south to Vanuatu/Austral Islands), taxonomy (Pomacentridae), temperature (25.4–28.4°C wild habitat, averaging with LiveAquaria range), and diet (planktivore/zooplankton). Temperature disagrees with LiveAquaria (22.2–25.6°C vs 25.4–28.4°C); averaged range 24–27°C applied.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous; males guard benthic egg masses and aerate eggs. Distinct pairing observed during spawning. Rarely bred successfully in home aquaria.
